overview: The image features a razor clam shell (specifically, likely a Solen or Ensis species) resting on a sandy beach. These bivalve mollusks are known for their long, narrow, straight shells that resemble old-fashioned straight razors. The pinkish coloration observed is consistent with certain varieties of razor shells often found after storms. They are common burrowing creatures that use a strong muscular "foot" to dig deep into sandy or muddy tidal substrates. The Wildlife Trusts +2 3 sites Razor shell | The Wildlife Trusts
